name: Stewed Paua (Nz)
recipeInstructions:
1. Remove the paua from its shell using a sharp knife.
Cut away the pewa or soft part of the paua, which can be used for soup.
Press out the two teeth from the mouth.
2. Dice the paua and onion.
3. Fry the onion in butter until it is cooked, then add the diced paua.
4. Cover with 2 cups boiling water and simmer gently until the paua is cooked.
5. Mix salt and flour with a little water to make a smooth paste.
Thicken the mixture. Serves 4.
SOURCE: Kiwi Cookbook, by Alan Armstrong, Seven Seas Publishing Pty Ltd, PO Box 1431, Wellington, New Zealand (C. 1968) ISBN 85467 016 5 SHARED BY: Jim Bodle 5/93
